findings.summary: Hand-assembled Class D audio amplifier Pmod board using 4x RS8754 quad comparator ICs driving LC output filters, with 16 op-amp channels configured as comparators. Analyzer correctly identifies the architecture and passive filtering.

correct.description: All 16 RS8754 op-amp units (U1-U4, 4 units each) correctly detected as comparator_or_open_loop configuration, with inverting inputs on channel A or B nets and non-inverting inputs on V1.6 reference rail
Two LC filters correctly detected: L2/C5+C12 and L1/C6+C13 both at 15.17 kHz resonant frequency with parallel capacitance of 11uF, representing the Class D output reconstruction filters
Decoupling analysis correctly identifies V1.6 rail (1uF on C14) and +5V rail (144uF across 9 capacitors including 100uF C4)
41 components correctly counted: 4 ICs, 18 resistors, 14 capacitors, 2 inductors, 3 connectors
Two power rails (+5V, V1.6) correctly identified as the main supply and reference voltage
J1 (Conn_01x06 Pmod connector), J2 (Conn_01x02), J4 connector correctly classified

missed.description: No voltage divider detected for generating the V1.6 reference rail from +5V. The V1.6 rail powers the non-inverting inputs of all comparators and has a 1uF decoupling cap (C14), but the divider or reference source that creates V1.6 from +5V was not identified. This may be generated by resistors R17/R18 or similar.

suggestions: The Class D amplifier topology (comparators + LC filter + feedback resistors) could be detected as a recognized signal chain pattern.
